issues.description: The Dockerfile uses wget for healthchecks but alpine-based images may not have wget installed by default. Should use curl or ensure wget is installed, or use a more robust health check method
issues.webResearchNote: The eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine image does not include wget by default. The HEALTHCHECK should either install wget (apk add --no-cache wget) or use a different approach like 'CMD-SHELL' with built-in tools.

issues.description: The example shows Maven wrapper usage but doesn't mention that the wrapper files must be executable (chmod +x mvnw) which can be a common issue in CI/CD environments
issues.webResearchNote: When copying the Maven wrapper into Docker, file permissions can be lost. The Dockerfile should handle this with 'RUN chmod +x mvnw' or use 'chmod 755' before build.

issues.description: The docker-compose.yml uses 'condition: service_healthy' in depends_on which requires Docker Compose v2.20+ or v3.4+ file format. The version '3.8' shown is correct but this feature may not work with older Docker Compose versions
issues.webResearchNote: Docker Compose v2.20+ (June 2023) added depends_on condition support. Earlier versions ignore conditions. This is worth noting for students with older Docker installations.

issues.description: Docker Compose v2 no longer requires the version declaration. While including it is backward compatible, the modern best practice is to omit the version line
issues.webResearchNote: Docker Compose v2 (the Go rewrite) uses the compose spec which doesn't require version. The version line is optional and ignored in newer versions.

issues.description: The workflow uses actions/setup-java@v4 with 'cache: maven' which requires the pom.xml to be at repository root. If the project is in a subdirectory (like java/ or backend/), caching won't work correctly
issues.webResearchNote: For monorepos or projects not at root, use 'cache-dependency-path: path/to/pom.xml' to specify the location.

issues.description: The Railway deployment example uses 'railway up' but doesn't mention that Railway v3 CLI requires a project ID or service ID. Students may encounter authentication/authorization errors
issues.webResearchNote: Railway CLI v3 requires 'RAILWAY_PROJECT_ID' or 'RAILWAY_SERVICE_ID' environment variables, or the --project flag. The lesson should mention this requirement.

issues.description: docker/build-push-action@v6 is now available with improved performance and features. While v5 works, upgrading to v6 is recommended
issues.webResearchNote: docker/build-push-action@v6 was released in early 2024. It includes better BuildKit support and improved caching. Not critical but worth updating.

issues.description: Railway's free tier and auto-detection behavior has changed. The lesson should note that Railway now requires a credit card for deployment even on the hobby plan
issues.webResearchNote: As of 2024-2025, Railway requires credit card verification for all deployments. The 'no credit card required' statement is outdated.

issues.originalText: We'll use Railway because: free tier for hobby projects
issues.description: Railway's free tier has been discontinued. The platform now requires payment for all usage. Alternative free options should be mentioned (Fly.io, Render free tier)
issues.webResearchNote: Railway ended their free tier in late 2023/early 2024. The course should update to mention Render, Fly.io, or Oracle Cloud Free Tier as alternatives.
